finding_results.notes: Replaced Loid's fallback-meal dialogue from 'There's rice in the cooker. And I made a small thing with the leftover chicken, in case you want options' to 'I cleaned up the leftover chicken — there's a plate on the counter.' This eliminates the rice/options cadence that echoed the calibration example ('I also made rice, in case you want... options'). The confirming narration at line 41 was updated from 'The backup rice sat discreetly on the stove for anyone who needed rescue' to 'The chicken plate sat on the counter for anyone who needed it' for consistency. The underlying behavior is preserved: Loid still quietly offers rescue food without embarrassing Yor. The new phrasing uses different syntax, sequencing, and lexicon — no reader could map it onto the style-bible calibration line.

summary: Single finding addressed. Loid's fallback-meal dialogue and its confirming narration were rewritten to break the structural echo with the style-bible calibration example. The new lines preserve the character behavior (discreet rescue food offered without calling attention to the stew) while using materially different verbal fingerprint — no rice, no 'options' landing, different sentence architecture. All non-negotiables maintained: heading contract intact, no clipping, no over-formality, no sanitization, character voices preserved.
